js_008: It’s about their arena — as a bird flies the sky, a fish swims the Sea.
js_009: What comes next in the pattern?\n🔴🔵🔵🔴🔵🔵🔴 ?
js_010: It repeats in groups of three 「🔴🔵🔵」 — after 🔴 comes 🔵.
js_016: The rest are fruit — only the Carrot is a vegetable.
js_018: The Fibonacci sequence — each number is the sum of the two before it. 5+8=13.
js_019: Rotate the lowercase letter ‘b’ 180° — what do you get?
js_020: It flips both vertically and horizontally, so it becomes q. (Mirror left-right only → d; flip top-bottom only → p)
js_023: A pencil and eraser cost $1.10 together.\nThe pencil costs $1.00 more than the eraser.\nHow much is the eraser?
js_028: eraser x, pencil x+100¢ → 2x+100=110 → x=5¢. (The intuitive 10¢ is the trap!)
js_031: All Mimis are Factories.\nSome Factories are plants.\nWhich must be true?
js_036: The first premise is the answer as-is. The rest are inferences that aren’t guaranteed — nothing says ‘some Factories’ includes any Mimi.
js_042: It’s about workplace — a judge works in a Court.
js_043: At 3:30 on a clock,\nwhat’s the angle between the hour and minute hands?
js_048: The minute hand is at 6 (180°), the hour hand halfway between 3 and 4 (105°) → the gap is 75°.
js_049: If today is Wednesday,\nwhat day is it 100 days later?
js_054: 100 ÷ 7 = 14 weeks + 2 days → 2 days after Wednesday is Friday.
js_055: 5 years ago, Dad was 4× the son’s age.\nIf Dad is now 45, how old is the son?
js_060: 5 years ago Dad was 40 = son ×4 → son was 10 → now 15.
js_061: On a die, opposite faces sum to 7.\nIf 1 is on top and 2 faces front,\nwhat number is on the right? (standard die)
js_062: On a standard die, 1·2·3 meet at one corner going clockwise → the right face is 3.
js_068: You nearly aced all 15 — your brain is in top condition today.
js_070: You cleared most of the traps too — your logic circuits are nicely warmed up.
js_072: Steady, above-average work! Review just the types you missed and you’re set.
js_074: Your brain’s still preheating. Grab a coffee, try again, and you might get a different result.
js_076: It’s not your score — it’s your condition! Come back with a clear head.
